1.text: An IP address is a unique numerical identifier assigned to each device on a network.

2.text: Latency is the time delay between sending and receiving data.

3.text: CPU stands for Central Processing Unit.

4.text: HTTP stands for Hyper Text Transfer Protocol.

5.text:
Ram is volatile, ROM is non volatile.
RAM:
It is used for temporary storage.
ROM:
It is used for permanent instructions.

6.text: A firewall is a network security system that monitors and controls incoming and outgoing traffic. It is used to protect systems from unauthorized access.

7.text: The Domain Name System (DNS) is used to translate human readable domain names to IP addresses. This helps computers locate servers.

8.text:
Load Balancing is the process of distributing incoming network traffic across multiple servers to ensure no single server is overloaded.
This improves system performance by sharing workload, ensures high availability and scalability.

9.text:
The OSI (Open System Interconnection) model is a conceptual framework used to understand how data is transmitted over a network.
Physical layer → Transmits raw bits
Data link layer → Handles error detection
Network Layer - Responsible for routing
Transport Layer → Ensures reliable data transfer.
Session Layer - Manages communication session

10.text: Data Transmission over the internet follows a structured process using networking protocols and devices.

aligned_pairs.student_text: An IP address is a unique numerical identifier assigned to each device on a network.
aligned_pairs.model_text: An IP address is a unique numerical identifier assigned to each device on a network.

aligned_pairs.student_text: Latency is the time delay between sending and receiving data.
aligned_pairs.model_text: Latency is the time delay between sending and receiving data.

aligned_pairs.student_text: CPU stands for Central Processing Unit.
aligned_pairs.model_text: CPU stands for Central Processing Unit.

aligned_pairs.student_text: HTTP stands for Hyper Text Transfer Protocol.
aligned_pairs.model_text: HTTP stands for HyperText Transfer Protocol.

aligned_pairs.student_text:
Ram is volatile, ROM is non volatile.
RAM:
It is used for temporary storage.
ROM:
It is used for permanent instructions.
aligned_pairs.model_text:
RAM is volatile memory and loses data when power is off, while ROM is non-volatile and retains data. RAM stores temporary data for active processing, whereas ROM stores permanent instructions like firmware. RAM is faster and easily modifiable, while ROM is slower and mostly read-only.
Reassembly:
At the receiver's end, packets are reassembled in the correct order to form the original data.

aligned_pairs.student_text: A firewall is a network security system that monitors and controls incoming and outgoing traffic. It is used to protect systems from unauthorized access.
aligned_pairs.model_text:
A firewall is a network security system that monitors and controls incoming and outgoing traffic based on security rules. It is used to protect systems from unauthorized access, malware, and cyber attacks.
Display:
The browser interprets and displays the data (webpage, video, etc.)

aligned_pairs.student_text: The Domain Name System (DNS) is used to translate human readable domain names to IP addresses. This helps computers locate servers.
aligned_pairs.model_text:
Explain the working of DNS in brief.
Ans: The Domain Name System (DNS) is used to translate human-readable domain names into IP addresses.
Working:
1. When a user enters a domain (e.g., google.com), the request is sent to a DNS resolver.
2. The resolver checks its cache; if not found, it queries root, TLD, and authoritative DNS servers.
3. The correct IP address is returned to the client.
4. The browser then uses this IP to connect to the web server.

aligned_pairs.student_text:
Load Balancing is the process of distributing incoming network traffic across multiple servers to ensure no single server is overloaded.
This improves system performance by sharing workload, ensures high availability and scalability.
aligned_pairs.model_text:
What is load balancing? Mention its importance.
Ans: Load balancing is the process of distributing incoming network traffic across multiple servers to ensure no single server is overloaded.
Importance:
• Improves system performance by sharing workload
• Ensures high availability (if one server fails, others handle requests)
• Enhances scalability by allowing more servers to be added
• Reduces response time for users

aligned_pairs.student_text:
The OSI (Open System Interconnection) model is a conceptual framework used to understand how data is transmitted over a network.
Physical layer → Transmits raw bits
Data link layer → Handles error detection
Network Layer - Responsible for routing
Transport Layer → Ensures reliable data transfer.
Session Layer - Manages communication session
aligned_pairs.model_text:
Explain the OSI model and list its layers.
Ans: The OSI (Open Systems Interconnection) model is a conceptual framework used to understand how data is transmitted over a network. It divides communication into seven layers, each with specific functions.

aligned_pairs.student_text: Data Transmission over the internet follows a structured process using networking protocols and devices.
aligned_pairs.model_text:
Explain how data is transmitted over the internet.
Ans: Data transmission over the internet follows a structured process using networking protocols and devices:
1. User Request:
The sender enters a URL in the browser. DNS translates it into an IP address.
2. Data Packet Formation:
The data is divided into smaller units called packets. Each packet contains:
Ο
Source IP
Ο Destination IP
Ο Data
3. Transmission via Network:
Packets travel through multiple routers and switches across different paths in the network.
4. Protocol Handling:
Protocols like:
Ο
TCP ensure reliable delivery (error checking, ordering)
